cards.back: The process of selecting a subset of relevant features from a large set of features to improve the performance of a machine learning model.
A dimensionality reduction technique that transforms a set of correlated features into a new set of uncorrelated features, called principal components.
A statistical measure that calculates the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two continuous variables.
A measure of the amount of information that one feature provides about another feature, often used in feature selection and dimensionality reduction.
The process of scaling numeric features to a common range, often to improve the stability and performance of machine learning algorithms.
